content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Let \(G\) be a group of order \(44\). Prove that

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: a Sylow \(11\)-subgroup of \(G\) is normal in \(G\), and

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: the centre of \(G\) contains an element of order \(2\).

body.text: Let \(G=A\times B\) be a finite group which is the (internal) direct product of two subgroups \(A\) and \(B\) which are nonabelian simple groups.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the only proper, nontrivial normal subgroups of \(G\) are \(A\) and \(B\). (Hint: If \(N\) is a proper normal subgroup different from \(A\) and \(B\), consider the commutator \([N,A]\).)

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example to show that (a) would be false were \(A\) and \(B\) allowed to be abelian.

body.text: Let \(G=\mathrm{GL}_2(\mathbb{R})\) act on \(\mathbb{R}^2\) by matrix multiplication: \(A\mathbin{\cdot}\mathbf{v}=A\mathbf{v}\) for \(A\in G\), \(\mathbf{v}\in\mathbb{R}^2\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: How many orbits are there for the action of \(G\) on \(\mathbb{R}^2\)?

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text:
Define the ascending central series
\[
Z_0(G)\leq Z_1(G)\leq Z_2(G)\leq\cdots
\]
for \(G\). (This is also known as the upper central series.)

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the groups \(Z_i(G)\) are all characteristic subgroups of \(G\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of a nontrivial group \(G\) such that the groups \(Z_i(G)\) are all trivial.

body.text:
Let \(n\geq 2\) and let \(Z_n\) be a cyclic group of order \(n\). Prove that
\[
\operatorname{Aut}(Z_n)\cong (\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z})^\times.
\]
